literal_meaning: Dispassion toward sense-objects, absence of egoism, and the constant seeing of the defects in birth, death, old age and disease.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the cognitive-emotional skills that turn mere data into liberating knowledge: the ability to step back from cravings, to drop the story of “I, me, mine,” and to keep the sobering facts of human vulnerability in daily awareness.
psychological_state: Mortality-salience with ego-decentering; the mind is trained to treat every sensory pull and life milestone as transient, reducing compulsive attachment.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling late-night through curated Instagram reels and suddenly noticing the creases on your parents’ faces in the next room—feeling both the ache of impermanence and the pull to keep swiping.
anchor_text: Notice cravings, drop the 'I' story, remember aging and death.
